description_long: This object is a vintage-style radio receiver with a rectangular body featuring a brushed metal top and front panel, accented by wooden side panels. The front face displays a horizontal blue-lit frequency indicator, a large round tuning knob, several smaller control knobs, and toggle switches, as well as a small analog meter. The top surface has two sets of ventilation slits for heat dissipation. The rear panel is equipped with multiple RCA input/output ports and speaker terminals, clearly labeled for left and right channels. The sides are plain, showcasing the wood grain finish. The overall design suggests a robust, high-fidelity audio component suitable for home or garage use.
description: A vintage radio receiver with a brushed metal front, blue-lit frequency display, wooden sides, and multiple control knobs and ports.
description_view_0: The front view shows the frequency indicator, analog meter, large tuning knob, several smaller control knobs, and toggle switches.
description_view_1: The left side view displays the plain wooden panel and the protruding control knobs from the front.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals multiple RCA ports, speaker terminals, and a black back panel framed by wooden sides.
description_view_3: The right side view shows the plain wooden panel and the ends of the control knobs from the front.
